title: cracca hispidula
intro: Cracca hispidula, also known as stick-tongue or rough cracca, is a flowering plant species native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is also believed to have health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating pain. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support the body's natural healing processes.
